profile_basis: Post-pass113 RCH fj-dispatch vmap profile on ts2 ranked vmap_eigh/batched_matrix_3x3 at 158.89us, vmap_svd/batched_matrix_3x2 at 111.00us, vmap_while/functional_sub_jaxpr_sub_two_128 at 91.44us, vmap_dot_i64/paired_vectors at 82.41us, and vmap_qr/batched_matrix_3x2 at 65.25us. frankenjax-ng9ky was the ready [perf] bead for the next register-blocked 3x2 SVD/linalg primitive.
lever: Unrolled bit-identical 3x2 thin SVD helper inside fj-dispatch batching for the exact m=3, n=2, full_matrices=false benchmark shape. It preserves the existing A^T A -> 2x2 Jacobi -> descending total_cmp sort -> U/S/Vt computation while avoiding per-slice matrix scratch and generic loops.
baseline_command: RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-ng9ky-svd-baseline cargo bench -p fj-dispatch --bench dispatch_baseline -- vmap_svd/batched_matrix_3x2 --sample-size 40 --measurement-time 2 --warm-up-time 1 --noplot
rebench_command: RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-ng9ky-svd-rebench cargo bench -p fj-dispatch --bench dispatch_baseline -- vmap_svd/batched_matrix_3x2 --sample-size 40 --measurement-time 2 --warm-up-time 1 --noplot
isomorphism_proof: The fast path is gated to m == 3, n == 2, and full_matrices == false inside the existing batch_svd_multi path. All other SVD modes and shapes continue to call svd_decompose_matrix exactly as before.
Accepted inputs are read in the same row-major order from the same tensor element slice. Nonnumeric elements still use Literal::as_f64 and return the same type-mismatch EvalError text.

The unrolled helper preserves the generic arithmetic for the 3x2 thin case: identical A^T A dot-product accumulation order, identical 2x2 Jacobi threshold and angle formulas, identical eigenvector update order, identical descending f64::total_cmp ordering with equal-value order preserved, identical sigma max/sqrt logic, identical U dot-product order, and identical Vt transpose layout.
The focused unit test compares U, S, and Vt f64::to_bits output against svd_decompose_matrix(3, 2, matrix, false) for benchmark-pattern, positive, mixed-sign, and all-zero matrices.
There is no RNG and no side effect in the accepted SVD fragment. Batch slice ordering and U/S/Vt output extension order are unchanged.
validation: RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-ng9ky-svd-tests cargo test -p fj-dispatch svd_ --lib -- --nocapture
RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-ng9ky-svd-check cargo check -p fj-dispatch --all-targets
RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-ng9ky-svd-libtests cargo test -p fj-dispatch --lib
RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-ng9ky-svd-clippy cargo clippy -p fj-dispatch --all-targets -- -D warnings
